channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India's PLI Scheme Powers $250B Mobile Output
item.description: India's mobile manufacturing output has surged to Rs 24 lakh crore ($250 billion) since fiscal 2020-21, driven by the government's production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme. The initiative has been highly efficient, costing less than 1% of production value by focusing on performance targets and global integration. It has boosted the manufacturing ecosystem, adding nearly Rs 1 lakh crore in GST, but an 11-14% cost disadvantage against China challenges export leadership.

item.title: Metal Stocks Trade Below Book: Maithan Alloys, Prakash Industries Offer Value
item.description: Niche metal manufacturers Maithan Alloys and Prakash Industries present a rare opportunity, trading below their book value despite profit growth and dividends. With low P/E ratios and significant discounts to net worth, these companies suggest a market disconnect and deep-value potential, offering tangible equity and strong yields.

item.title: Indian Travel Boom Hits Record Highs, But Online Agencies Face Profit Squeeze
item.description: India's travel sector is booming, with record outbound trips and accelerating domestic travel. While this surge boosts online travel aggregators (OTAs), it hides steep margin pressures. Companies like Easy Trip Planners report profit drops despite revenue growth, while Ixigo and TBO Tek struggle with high valuations and competition. IRCTC shows a more balanced profile. The main hurdle is turning high booking volumes into lasting profits.

item.title: India's EV Market Roars: Mid-Range Boom Fueled by Value Seekers, Sales Jump 77%
item.description: India's electric passenger vehicle (PV) market saw sales surge 77% year-on-year in 2025 to 175,000 units. The growth is concentrated in the ₹10-30 lakh segment, driven by consumers seeking advanced technology and better value, rather than just fuel price volatility. While major players expand, entry-level and premium segments face distinct hurdles.
